content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactual Contrastive Prompting to Mitigate Social Bias in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models frequently exhibit social biases and reinforce stereotypes in generated text, even when prompts are neutral or ambiguous. Current prompting techniques only partially reduce such biases.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are deployed in more societal-facing applications, unchecked biases can cause real-world harm and perpetuate discrimination. Despite fine-tuning and alignment efforts, prompting remains a key tool for real-time mitigation, but more effective and principled methods are urgently needed.",
  "method": "We propose a novel 'Counterfactual Contrastive Prompting' (CCP) method: for each target prompt, we automatically generate a set of counterfactual prompts by swapping demographic variables (e.g., gender, ethnicity, age) and then prepend an instruction requiring symmetric, stereotype-neutral outputs across variants. The LLM is prompted to explicitly compare its completions for each variant, identify asymmetries, and revise its answer to ensure parity. This approach leverages the model’s in-context ability to self-critique and adjust, reducing bias without additional finetuning or external classifiers.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate CCP on Social Bias Benchmarks (e.g., StereoSet, CrowS-Pairs) and compare against standard, rephrased, and instruction-based prompting baselines. Bias metrics (e.g., Stereotype Score, Language Modeling Bias) and human evaluation will be used to assess stereotype reduction and answer quality."
}
